ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) DNS-tunnel C2 — BONDUPDATER / Helminth / ALMA Communicator beacon traffic
ucs.description: Hunts the high-cardinality, encoded-subdomain DNS pattern that OilRig's signature implants (BONDUPDATER, Helminth, ALMA Communicator, DNSpionage) use to exfil and receive tasking over DNS A/TXT records. Catches a single endpoint resolving dozens of unique long, hex/base32-looking subdomains under one parent zone — the canonical OilRig DNS-tunnel fingerprint documented by FireEye, Palo Alto Unit 42 and Cisco Talos.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count as queries values(DNS.query) as sample_queries dc(DNS.query) as unique_subs values(DNS.src) as src values(DNS.record_type) as rtypes from datamodel=Network_Resolution where DNS.message_type="QUERY" NOT DNS.query IN ("*.microsoft.com","*.windows.com","*.windowsupdate.com","*.office.com","*.office365.com","*.azure.com","*.akamai*","*.googleapis.com","*.cloudfront.net","*.amazonaws.com") by DNS.src host _time span=10m DNS.query | rex field=DNS.query "^(?<label>[^.]+)\.(?<parent>[^.]+\.[^.]+)$" | stats sum(queries) as queries dc(label) as unique_labels values(rtypes) as rtypes values(sample_queries) as sample_queries by DNS.src host parent _time | eval avg_label_len=round(unique_labels/queries,2) | where unique_labels>=40 AND queries>=80 AND (mvfind(rtypes,"TXT")>=0 OR mvfind(rtypes,"A")>=0) | sort - unique_labels
ucs.kql:
// APT34 / OilRig DNS-tunnel beacon — BONDUPDATER / Helminth / DNSpionage
// Look for one host resolving many high-entropy subdomains under a single parent zone
let _known_zones = dynamic(["microsoft.com","windows.com","windowsupdate.com","office.com","office365.com","azure.com","akamaiedge.net","googleapis.com","cloudfront.net","amazonaws.com","trafficmanager.net","msftncsi.com","sharepoint.com"]);
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(24h)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend Q = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName))
| where isnotempty(Q)
| extend parent = strcat(tostring(split(Q, ".")[-2]), ".", tostring(split(Q, ".")[-1]))
| where parent !in (_known_zones)
| extend label = tostring(split(Q, ".")[0])
| extend label_len = strlen(label)
| where label_len >= 12      // OilRig DNS labels are typically >=20 chars (encoded chunks)
| summarize Queries = count(),
            UniqueLabels = dcount(label),
            MaxLabelLen = max(label_len),
            AvgLabelLen = avg(label_len),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            SampleQueries = make_set(Q, 5),
            Processes = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 5)
            by DeviceName, DeviceId, parent
| where UniqueLabels >= 40 and Queries >= 80      // tunnel volume floor
| extend BeaconWindowMin = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where BeaconWindowMin >= 10                      // sustained, not a one-shot
| order by UniqueLabels desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT34 / OilRig DNS-tunnel C2 — Sentinel via Windows DNS server logs
let _known_zones = dynamic(["microsoft.com","windows.com","windowsupdate.com","office.com","office365.com","azure.com","akamaiedge.net","googleapis.com","cloudfront.net","amazonaws.com","trafficmanager.net","msftncsi.com","sharepoint.com"]);
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where isnotempty(Name)
| extend Q = tolower(Name)
| extend parent = strcat(tostring(split(Q, ".")[-2]), ".", tostring(split(Q, ".")[-1]))
| where parent !in (_known_zones)
| extend label = tostring(split(Q, ".")[0])
| extend label_len = strlen(label)
| where label_len >= 12
| summarize Queries = count(),
            UniqueLabels = dcount(label),
            MaxLabelLen = max(label_len),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            QueryTypes = make_set(QueryType, 5),
            SampleQueries = make_set(Q, 5)
            by ClientIP, Computer, parent
| where UniqueLabels >= 40 and Queries >= 80
| extend BeaconWindowMin = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where BeaconWindowMin >= 10
| order by UniqueLabels desc
ucs.rationale: OilRig has used DNS as a primary C2 channel since 2016 and continues to do so (BONDUPDATER, Helminth, ALMA Communicator, DNSpionage, Karkoff, Saitama). Their tradecraft is unmistakable: each beacon is encoded into a long subdomain label (often >20 chars hex/base32), and infected hosts resolve many distinct labels under a single attacker-controlled parent within minutes. Generic exfil hunts look fo

ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) IIS web-shell post-exploitation — TwoFace / RGDoor / HighShell command spawn from w3wp.exe
ucs.description: Detects OilRig's signature post-exploit pattern after they drop their IIS-resident web shells (TwoFace, RGDoor, HighShell, HyperShell, Karkoff loader) on Exchange/OWA/IIS servers — namely w3wp.exe (or httpd.exe) spawning shell/recon binaries. This is the precise tradecraft documented in the APT34 leaks (Lab Dookhtegan / Read My Lips, 2019) and Mandiant/FireEye investigations of Middle East telecoms and government compromises.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as children dc(Processes.process_name) as unique_children from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="httpd.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="tomcat*.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","hostname.exe","ping.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe") by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user Processes.process_name | rename Processes.* as * | where unique_children>=2 OR match(cmdlines,"(?i)(whoami|net\s+user|net\s+group|nltest|systeminfo|-enc|EncodedCommand|DownloadString|Invoke-Expression|certutil\s+-(?:urlcache|decode))") | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT34 / OilRig — IIS web-shell command execution (TwoFace / RGDoor / HighShell)
let _recon_bins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","hostname.exe","ping.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe"]);
let _shell_bins  = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe"]);
let _download_bins = dynamic(["bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"          // IIS worker — TwoFace/RGDoor live here
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (_shell_bins) or FileName in~ (_recon_bins) or FileName in~ (_download_bins)
| extend SuspectCmd = case(
    ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b(whoami|net\s+(user|group|localgroup)|nltest|systeminfo|tasklist|quser|ipconfig\s*/all|arp\s+-a)\b", "recon",
    ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(-enc(odedcommand)?|FromBase64String|DownloadString|DownloadFile|Invoke-Expression|IEX\s|Net\.WebClient)", "ps_download",
    ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(certutil(\.exe)?\s+-(urlcache|decode|decodehex)|bitsadmin(\.exe)?\s+/transfer)", "lolbin_download",
    "shell_spawn")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd     = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentSHA256  = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          ChildProcess  = FileName,
          ChildCmd      = ProcessCommandLine,
          SuspectCmd,
          ChildIntegrity = ProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT34 / OilRig — IIS web-shell spawn pattern via Windows 4688
let _recon_bins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","hostname.exe","ping.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe"]);
let _shell_bins  = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe"]);
let _download_bins = dynamic(["bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
let _interesting = array_concat(_recon_bins, _shell_bins, _download_bins);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in~ (_interesting)
| extend SuspectCmd = case(
    CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b(whoami|net\s+(user|group|localgroup)|nltest|systeminfo|tasklist|quser|ipconfig\s*/all|arp\s+-a)\b", "recon",
    CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(-enc(odedcommand)?|FromBase64String|DownloadString|DownloadFile|Invoke-Expression|IEX\s|Net\.WebClient)", "ps_download",
    CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(certutil(\.exe)?\s+-(urlcache|decode|decodehex)|bitsadmin(\.exe)?\s+/transfer)", "lolbin_download",
    "shell_spawn")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ChildBin, SuspectCmd, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT34 OilRig IIS Web-Shell Command Execution
id: 7c6a4b1f-3d4d-4af9-9f0a-8b2e2c1f4a91
status: experimental
description: Detects OilRig (APT34) post-exploit activity from IIS web shells (TwoFace, RGDoor, HighShell) where w3wp.exe spawns shell, recon, or LOLBin download utilities.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0049/
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2019/04/apt34-oilrig-leak.html
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/the-curious-case-of-a-fileless-tinypos-variant/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1087.002
  - attack.g0049
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
  - IntegrityLevel
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IIS-hosted management consoles invoking diagnostic commands
  - Vendor monitoring agents that probe via the web-app pool identity
level: high
ucs.rationale: OilRig's playbook against Middle East government, energy and telecom victims has consistently been: spear-phish for entry, then pivot to internet-facing IIS / OWA / Exchange and drop ASP/ASPX web shells (TwoFace and its TwoFace Loader variant, RGDoor as backup IIS module, HighShell, HyperShell). Once in, they drive the web shell to run cmd/powershell with classic recon (whoami, net user/group, nlt
